title: Computer Systems Design Services

alternatives.name: Project-led computer systems integrator
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.

Project-based planning, design, configuration, implementation, or integration of computer hardware, packaged software, networks, security systems, and communication technologies is the firm's largest revenue category.
The firm may also provide support, managed services, consulting, or product resale, but none of those categories exceeds project-based systems integration revenue.
exclusion_criteria: Recurring contracted management or technical support is the largest revenue category.
Stand-alone advisory, staff augmentation, or technical consulting is the largest revenue category.
Software publishing, intellectual-property licensing, hardware or software resale, hosting, or infrastructure provisioning is the largest revenue category.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 44% of the derived 3,524-firm band, rounded to the nearest firm. The 44% share is a dated, broader-channel survey estimate for firms naming IT solutions projects combining hardware, software, and services as their leading revenue source. Its ordering is corroborated, but not directly measured at the firm level, by the 2022 Economic Census: reported 541512 revenue in packaged-software customization/integration, network design/security, systems design/development, and systems integration totals about $153.9B, versus about $24.4B in infrastructure/network management, technical support, and hardware maintenance. Product lines can coexist within an establishment, and all-size revenue is not a target-band firm-count measure, so the supported count range is deliberately wide.

alternatives.name: Managed-services-led IT provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Recurring contracted IT infrastructure or network management, monitoring, maintenance, help-desk, or technical support is the firm's largest revenue category.
The firm may undertake systems-integration projects, consulting, or resale, but recurring managed and support services exceed each of those categories.
exclusion_criteria: Project-based systems design or integration is the largest revenue category.
Stand-alone advisory, staff augmentation, or technical consulting is the largest revenue category.
Software publishing, licensing, product resale, hosting, or infrastructure provisioning is the largest revenue category.
The establishment primarily performs on-site management and operation of a client's computer systems or data-processing facilities, which the NAICS definition assigns to 541513 rather than 541512.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 30% of the derived target band, rounded to the nearest firm, using a broader-channel survey estimate for managed services as the leading revenue source. Census reports 10,640 541512 establishments with IT infrastructure/network management revenue and 20,604 with IT technical-support revenue, but those establishment counts overlap and do not show which line is primary. Trade and association evidence also treats MSP, VAR, consultant, and systems-integrator labels as overlapping identities. The range therefore allows substantial hybrid reclassification and does not treat MSP as synonymous with NAICS 541512.

alternatives.name: IT consulting- or staffing-led specialist
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Stand-alone IT technical consulting, assessment, architecture advice, project management, or staff augmentation is the firm's largest revenue category.
Implementation may follow the advice, but project integration, recurring management/support, and products each contribute less revenue than consulting and staffing.
exclusion_criteria: Project-based systems design or integration is the largest revenue category.
Recurring managed or technical-support services are the largest revenue category.
Software publishing, licensing, product resale, hosting, or infrastructure provisioning is the largest revenue category.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 14% of the derived target band, a residual allocation after the two survey-anchored leading models and the separately bounded product-led model. The 2022 Economic Census reports 18,281 541512 establishments and about $18.8B of revenue associated with IT technical consulting, confirming a material consulting line but not primary-model firm counts. The low and high cases allow for consulting embedded in project firms and for firms using consultant labels while earning most revenue from another model.

alternatives.name: Product-, IP-, hosting-, or resale-led solution provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Software publishing, intellectual-property licensing, hosting or infrastructure provisioning, or integrated hardware and software resale is the firm's largest revenue category.
Systems-integration services remain sufficient for the firm to be classified in 541512 rather than wholesale, retail, software publishing, or data-processing industries.
exclusion_criteria: Project-based systems design or integration is the largest revenue category.
Recurring managed or technical-support services are the largest revenue category.
Stand-alone consulting or staff augmentation is the largest revenue category.
The firm only sells hardware or software without systems-integration services, which the NAICS definition assigns outside 541512.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 7% of the derived target band. Census identifies material 541512 revenue from application software publishing, hosting, infrastructure provisioning, licensing, and some hardware/software sales, while the NAICS boundary requires integration services when products are sold. Product incidence does not reveal the firm's primary model, and channel surveys show frequent resale by service firms, so a comparatively small base and a wide range are used.

residual.name: Mixed, tied, or unclassified 541512 target-band firms
residual.rationale: Base residual is 5% of the 3,524-firm band, rounded so archetype bases plus residual equal the band total. It retains firms with tied or unobservable primary revenue categories, unusual combinations, or insufficient evidence for assignment. The high case recognizes that hybrid business models and multi-product Census reporting can prevent reliable primary-model classification.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: 541512 Computer Systems Design Services
Economic Census: Products by Industry Statistics for the U.S.
2022 Economic Census Products by Industry downloadable dataset
CompTIA Releases 5th Annual Trends in Managed Services
sources.vintage: 2016 secondary summary of CompTIA channel survey

sources.title: 2022 State of the Channel: Happy Days Are Here Again
National Society of IT Service Providers: About

target_archetype.selection_basis: The four named archetypes assign hybrid firms by their single largest revenue category, making the options mutually exclusive; unresolved ties remain in the residual. Applied to the derived 3,524-firm band, broader-channel leading-revenue shares give 1,551 project-led firms (44%) and 1,057 managed-services-led firms (30%). The difference is 494 firms, or 31.9% of the selected base, so there is no near tie on base counts. The project-led ranking is directionally corroborated by 2022 Census product revenue, where the enumerated design and integration lines total about $153.9B. Project-led is therefore the objective largest-base selection. The enumeration covers 3,348 firms at base, or 95.0% of the band, with the remaining 176 firms named as residual.

value_basis.scope: Cash operating costs of a project-led computer systems integrator in the derived $1-10M EBITDA band; role weights allocate only the labor-and-contractor cash-cost pool and are mutually exclusive.

labor_contractor_cash_cost_share.rationale: A bounded, class-neutral structural assumption is frozen because the selected model necessarily combines labor-intensive design and delivery with potentially material third-party hardware, software, cloud, and subcontractor pass-through. The range is intentionally wide enough to include asset-light consulting-like integrators and product-heavy solution providers that could be reclassified into the selected archetype. It is not an empirical estimate for the target archetype.
caveats: The derived six-digit industry labor share is not used as target-archetype evidence.
Third-party product and cloud pass-through can move the share materially across firms and projects.
Contractors are included with labor; purchased products and hosted infrastructure are excluded.
Selection uncertainty requires retaining the full range rather than narrowing around the base.

roles.title: Solution architecture and systems design
roles.rationale: Assumed share of the labor-and-contractor pool for discovery, requirements, solution architecture, security and network design, and technical specifications; excludes hands-on implementation and sales engineering booked to other roles.

roles.title: Implementation, configuration, and integration delivery
roles.rationale: Assumed share for engineers and contractors configuring, installing, integrating, migrating, and deploying client systems; excludes architecture, project management, and post-deployment support.

roles.title: Project delivery management and quality assurance
roles.rationale: Assumed share for scoping control, scheduling, coordination, documentation, testing governance, and delivery quality; excludes technical build work and corporate management.

roles.title: Deployment support and user training
roles.rationale: Assumed share for go-live support, incident resolution tied to delivered systems, knowledge transfer, and user training; excludes recurring infrastructure management that would determine the managed-services-led archetype.

roles.title: Sales, solution pursuit, and account management
roles.rationale: Assumed share for business development, proposal coordination, partner and vendor liaison, and account stewardship; excludes billable architecture and implementation.

roles.title: Executive, finance, people, compliance, and internal operations
roles.rationale: Assumed share for firm leadership and non-delivery operations, including finance, contracting, human resources, compliance, facilities, and internal systems; excludes project delivery management.

value_basis.rationale: The available occupation mix is published only for broad NAICS 5415 and is structurally mismatched to the selected project-led 541512 archetype. No evidence bridge supports deterministic target-specific reweighting, so that proxy is rejected. A bounded assumption basis is frozen instead: six mutually exclusive role groups sum to 1.00 of labor-and-contractor cash cost, with no source claim and no upgrade from ASSUMPTION/NONE.
caveats: This basis forces readiness to UNPROVEN and must not be represented as observed or proxy evidence.
Role weights are structural allocation assumptions, not occupation employment shares or wage shares.
The role set applies only to the selected project-led archetype, not all 541512 firms.
Do not substitute the broad 5415 occupation mix or an MSP staffing model.
Subcontracted delivery must be assigned to the role describing the work so it is not double counted.

source_hints.source: Project-level case studies, systems-integration statements of work, implementation benchmarks, and professional-association operating surveys specific to mid-market project-led integrators
source_hints.why: Needed to distinguish architecture, build, project-control, support, and training work and to establish implementation timing and constraints for the selected archetype.

source_hints.source: Audited segment disclosures, lender or transaction memoranda, and channel surveys reporting project backlog, repeat-client revenue, recurring support, resale pass-through, and customer concentration
source_hints.why: Project repeat business, recurring support, and pass-through products have different retention and cash-cost implications; generic MSP retention is not transferable.

source_hints.source: Closed transaction databases and broker or investment-bank deal reports filtered to U.S. project-led systems integrators with approximately $1-10M EBITDA
source_hints.why: Industry-wide technology-services transaction counts may combine software, staffing, consulting, VAR, and MSP models and must be narrowed to the selected archetype and size band.

source_hints.source: Closed-deal purchase-price and EBITDA records with revenue-mix, working-capital, customer-concentration, and owner-dependence fields
source_hints.why: Project-led integrator multiples can depend on backlog quality, vendor concentration, product pass-through, and key-person risk; headline technology-services multiples may not transfer.

source_hints.source: Systems-integrator governance studies, certification and partner-program requirements, customer-contract change-of-control terms, and post-acquisition operating case studies
source_hints.why: Needed to test whether architecture authority, client trust, vendor status, and delivery oversight can persist without the selling owner at year five.

implementation: For project-led systems integrators in the target band, what directly observed workflow evidence separates solution architecture, implementation, project control, quality assurance, deployment support, and training costs?

What implementation durations, sequencing dependencies, security controls, vendor certifications, client-access constraints, and error-remediation requirements are supported by target-specific evidence?
Can five-year realization endpoints be bounded with evidence that applies to project-led integrators rather than software publishers, pure consultants, or managed-services-led firms?
commercial_retention: What share of gross profit, rather than gross billings, comes from repeat projects, recurring support, managed services, resale margins, and first-time implementation work?
How do backlog conversion, project renewal, client concentration, vendor dependence, fixed-price exposure, and product pass-through affect commercial retention for the selected archetype?
Is any retention evidence reported for the target EBITDA band and primary-revenue definition, or does it require an explicit size or business-model bridge?
sale_availability: How many U.S. project-led systems integrators in the target EBITDA band were marketed and sold in a defined recent period?
Can transaction evidence exclude pure MSPs, staffing firms, software companies, wholesalers, and firms outside the target band while preserving a documented denominator?
What evidence supports the low and high endpoints for annual target availability after accounting for add-ons, repeat sales, and undisclosed deal size?
valuation_robustness: What closed-deal EBITDA multiples apply to project-led systems integrators in the target band, and how do those multiples vary with recurring revenue, backlog, client and vendor concentration, product pass-through, and owner dependence?
Are reported earnings normalized consistently for contractor costs, owner compensation, working capital, and resale revenue?
What evidence supports both entry and downside-exit endpoints without importing software-company or MSP multiples?
operator_survival: Which sales, architecture, vendor-partner, delivery-governance, and client-escalation duties remain concentrated in the owner for target-band project-led integrators?
What observed succession or post-acquisition evidence shows whether those duties can be transferred to a non-owner operator by year five?
Do client contracts, certifications, security clearances, partner statuses, or change-of-control provisions create target-specific survival constraints?

special_notes: This record describes only the selected project-led systems-integrator archetype and must not be presented as the score or economics of all NAICS 541512 firms.
MSP is not a synonym for NAICS 541512. Managed-services-led firms are a separate alternative, and on-site facilities management belongs in NAICS 541513 under the official definition.
Hybrid firms are assigned by their single largest revenue category; exact ties and firms with unobservable revenue mix remain in the residual.
Economic Census product-line establishment counts overlap because an establishment may report multiple products; they must not be summed as unique firms.
Selection uncertainty is true because the project-led and managed-services-led supported count ranges overlap and no target-band primary-revenue census exists.
The broad 5415 occupation mix is rejected as a deterministic role proxy for the selected archetype. The frozen assumption value basis has no evidentiary weight and forces UNPROVEN readiness.
The count allocation is a class-neutral pre-research estimate: base counts sum to the derived 3,524-firm band, and the named alternatives cover 95% at base with an 80%-98% supported coverage range.
